title: [Phase 1] Initialize Repository
body:
## What
Set up a clean git repository with proper configuration files and documentation.

## Why
Establishes the foundation for the AI Trends Dashboard project.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Local git repository initialized
- [ ] `.gitignore` configured for Python projects
- [ ] `README.md` written with setup instructions
- [ ] Initial commit made
- [ ] Repository pushed to GitHub

## Implementation Plan
1. Run `git init`
2. Create `.gitignore` with Python patterns
3. Create comprehensive `README.md`
4. Make initial commit
5. Push to GitHub (https://github.com/ehdwns3755-byte/AI-)

## Estimated Effort
2 hours

## Related Issues
Blocks: #2, #3

title: [Phase 1] Create requirements.txt
body:
## What
Define Python dependencies for the project.

## Why
Allows team members to quickly set up identical development environments.

## Dependencies
- requests (HTTP library for API calls)
- beautifulsoup4 (HTML parsing)
- feedparser (RSS feed parsing)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `requirements.txt` created with correct versions
- [ ] All packages install without errors
- [ ] Tested in clean virtual environment

## Estimated Effort
1 hour

## Related Issues
Depends on: #1
Blocks: #3

title: [Phase 2] Implement News Collection (Google News, Hacker News, Product Hunt, Reddit)
body:
## What
Implement core news fetching from 4 sources.

## Why
Provides diverse AI news coverage for the dashboard.

## Sources
1. **Google News** - RSS feed
2. **Hacker News** - API (top stories)
3. **Product Hunt** - API (tech products)
4. **Reddit** - Web scraping (r/MachineLearning, r/LanguageModels)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 sources return news items
- [ ] Data stored in `ai_trends_data.json`
- [ ] Duplicate detection works
- [ ] Minimum 40 unique articles collected
- [ ] Error handling for API failures

## Implementation Plan
1. Create `ai_trends_dashboard.py` main class
2. Implement `fetch_google_news()` using feedparser
3. Implement `fetch_hacker_news()` using API
4. Implement `fetch_product_hunt()` using API
5. Implement `fetch_reddit()` using PRAW or requests
6. Combine results and save to JSON

## Estimated Effort
4 hours

## Related Issues
Depends on: #1, #2
Blocks: #4, #5

title: [Phase 2] Generate Responsive HTML Dashboard
body:
## What
Create a visually appealing HTML dashboard to display collected news.

## Why
Provides a clean interface for users to browse AI news.

## Features
- Responsive design (mobile, tablet, desktop)
- Dark mode support
- News items as readable cards
- Links to original sources
- Category tags (LLM, Vision, Automation, etc.)
- Auto-refresh capability (meta refresh)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HTML file `ai_dashboard.html` generates correctly
- [ ] Responsive design works on all screen sizes
- [ ] Dark mode toggles properly
- [ ] All news items visible with clickable links
- [ ] Renders in Chrome, Firefox, Safari
- [ ] Page loads in <2 seconds

## Estimated Effort
3 hours

## Related Issues
Depends on: #3
Blocks: #6

title: [Phase 2] Implement Duplicate Detection & Data Processing
body:
## What
Remove duplicate news and organize by date and category.

## Why
Improves dashboard quality by preventing duplicate articles and organizing content.

## Features
- Title-based duplicate detection
- Sort by date (newest first)
- Basic category classification

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Exact title matches are detected and removed
- [ ] Only first occurrence is kept
- [ ] Articles sorted chronologically
- [ ] Deduplication reduces count by 10-20%

## Implementation Plan
1. Implement `_remove_duplicates()` method
2. Use set for O(1) lookup
3. Add sorting by date
4. Log deduplication statistics

## Estimated Effort
2 hours

## Related Issues
Depends on: #3
Blocks: #4

title: [Phase 3] Set up Windows Task Scheduler Automation
body:
## What
Create PowerShell script to configure Windows Task Scheduler for daily 8 AM execution.

## Why
Enables fully automated news collection without manual intervention.

## Features
- Daily execution at 8:00 AM
- Logging to file
- Error handling
- Uninstall option

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `setup_scheduler.ps1` script created
- [ ] Scheduled task registers successfully
- [ ] Task runs daily at 8 AM
- [ ] Output logged to `ai_trends.log`
- [ ] Tested on Windows 10/11

## Implementation Plan
1. Create `setup_scheduler.ps1`
2. Configure ScheduledTaskAction
3. Set up daily trigger at 8 AM
4. Configure logging
5. Create uninstall function
6. Test execution

## Estimated Effort
2 hours

## Related Issues
Depends on: #3, #4, #5
Blocks: #7

title: [Phase 3] Complete Testing & Documentation
body:
## What
Test the complete system and finalize documentation.

## Why
Ensures reliability and provides clear setup instructions for users.

## Testing Checklist
- [ ] Manual script execution produces HTML
- [ ] Scheduled task runs at correct time
- [ ] HTML displays in browser correctly
- [ ] Logs are generated with expected format
- [ ] All features work as expected

## Documentation
- [ ] README.md updated with complete setup steps
- [ ] Troubleshooting guide added
- [ ] Screenshots of final dashboard
- [ ] Installation instructions verified

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All manual tests pass
- [ ] Scheduled automation verified working
- [ ] Documentation complete and clear
- [ ] Repository ready for public use

## Estimated Effort
2 hours

## Related Issues
Depends on: #6
Completes: v1.0
